docs.title: NLP Control Center Manual
docs.text: The NLP Control Center app is designed to simplify the configuration, monitoring, and maintenance of TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System) receivers and sensors.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Detecting and connecting to nearby receivers via BLE.
  • Updating firmware and customizing settings.
  • Diagnosing device issues and viewing sensor details.

docs.title: Devices Screen
docs.text:
The Devices Screen provides the following information for each detected receiver:

  • Signal Strength (RSSI): Bars and numerical values indicating proximity.
  • Device Name: Custom or default names, such as "NLP200" or "LRX200."
  • MAC Address: Matches the label on the receiver for identification.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Filtering Devices: Quickly find devices by filtering their names.
  • Connecting to a Device: Tap a device from the list or scan the receiver’s QR code for direct connection.
Devices Screen

docs.title: Installing the App
docs.text:
1. Download the app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.
  2. Enable Bluetooth and location permissions on your device to ensure connectivity.

Restricted Access Phase

The app is currently in a restricted access phase and is only available to authorized users. To get access: Provide us with the email address associated with your Google Play or Apple App Store account. Once we add you to the access list, you’ll be able to download the app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

docs.title: Launching the App
docs.text: Launch the app to access the Devices Screen, where all nearby TPMS receivers within BLE range will be displayed.

docs.title: In-Cab Display Configuration
docs.text: The In-Cab Display can also be configured through this app to work seamlessly with a truck receiver. Below is a guide to setting up and configuring the display:

docs.title: Linking the In-Cab Display to a Truck Receiver
docs.text:
Device Identification:

  • The in-cab display will appear in the Devices Screen with the name DRX110.
  • Select the DRX110 device to connect.

Access to Setup:

  • Once connected and on the Setup Screen you can update the device firmware or configure its settings.

docs.title: In-Cab Display Settings
docs.text:
The following settings can be configured for the in-cab display:

Number of Trailers:

  • Set the number of trailers the display should support.
  • Adjustable from 0 to 7 trailers.

Pressure Unit:

  • Choose the pressure unit to be displayed, such as bar or psi.

Temperature Unit:

  • Set the temperature unit, either Celsius (°C) or Fahrenheit (°F).

Audio Alarm:

  • Enable or disable the audio alarm.
  • When enabled, the display will emit an alarm sound during warnings or alerts.

Sleep Time:

  • Configure how quickly the display should turn off when inactive.
  • This helps conserve power.

docs.title: Linking the Display to a Truck Unit
docs.text:
After configuring the settings, the in-cab display must be linked to a truck receiver for full functionality:

Add the Truck MAC ID:

  • Use the app to enter the truck unit’s MAC ID.
  • Input methods:
  • Typing: Manually enter the MAC ID in the provided field.
  • QR Code: Scan the QR code associated with the truck unit to automatically input the MAC ID.
In-Cab Display Device Name In-Cab Display Device Name

docs.title: Monitor Screen
docs.text: The Monitor Screen provides real-time updates and status information about the connected device and sensors. It adapts its display based on the communication type (Filtered or Unfiltered). Below are the details for both modes:

docs.title: Vehicle Specific Data
docs.text:
For Filtered Devices, the Monitor Screen displays vehicle-specific data and tire sensor information in an organized layout.

  • Shows the type of vehicle (e.g., truck or trailer) currently being monitored.
  • Displays the name of the vehicle for easy identification.

docs.title: Trailer-Specific Details
docs.text:
• If the trailer is equipped with an Airline Sensor, the status and pressure of the airline will be displayed.
  • If the trailer has an Odometer setup, the current odometer value will also be visible.

docs.title: Sensor and Tire Data
docs.text: The lower section shows the vehicle layout with the data received from the connected sensors. Each tire displays its current pressure or temperature value above it.

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text:
• Green: Indicates everything is operating within normal thresholds.
  • Yellow: Represents a warning state requiring attention.
  • Red: Signals a critical issue that must be addressed immediately.

docs.title: Tire Details and Warnings
docs.text:
By clicking on a tire, you can access more detailed information, including:

  • Sensor ID
  • Sensor type
  • Active warnings or issues

This allows for quick diagnostics and resolution of any problems.

docs.title: Multiple Vehicle Support (Truck with Trailers)
docs.text: If a truck has one or more connected trailers, you can swipe horizontally to view the data for each vehicle in the chain.

docs.title: Unfiltered Devices
docs.text: For Unfiltered Devices, the Monitor Screen does not show a vehicle layout. Instead, it displays a simple list of received sensor data:

docs.title: Sensor Information List
docs.text:
When a sensor is detected, the following information is displayed in the list:

  • Sensor ID
  • Sensor type
  • Pressure value
  • Temperature value
  • Signal Strength

This mode provides a raw, unstructured view of sensor data without vehicle-specific configurations.

Filtered Device (all OK and Trailer) Filtered Device (warning) Tire Details Sensor List (unfiltered device)

docs.title: App Settings
docs.text:
Customize your app preferences:

  • Units: Choose your preffered pressure (PSI/Bar/kPa) and temperature (Celsius/Fahrenheit) units.
  • Default Sensor Type: Specify the default sensor type for later vehicle setups.
  • R141 TPMS Compliance: Enable or disable regulatory compliance features.

R141 support

Please talk to your distributor for details!

App Settings Menu

docs.title: Device Configuration
docs.text:
The Device Menu enables in-depth configurations:

  • Communication Types:

Each communication type utilizes a specific transmission method to send the data:

BLE (2.4GHz): Transmits data wirelessly using BLE technology. CAN (Controller Area Network): Communicates over a CAN bus, commonly used in automotive environments. RS232: Transmits data through a serial connection.

Type Mode Description BLE Filtered Allows setup of a vehicle and its tires, only displays/transmits sensors for this setup. Unfiltered Displays/Transmits all sensors around without a vehicle layout. CAN Filtered Allows setup of a vehicle and its tires, only displays/transmits sensors for this setup. Unfiltered Displays/Transmits any sensor data without a specific layout. CAN Garage A specific CAN mode tailored for garage or workshop environments. EBS A specific CAN mode tailored for Trailer EBS systems. Allows setup of a trailer and its tires, only displays/transmits sensors for this setup. RAW Custom communication types tailored for specific use cases. J1939 A specific CAN mode tailored for J1939 systems. Allows setup of a trailer and its tires, only displays/transmits sensors for this setup. RS232 Filtered Allows setup of a vehicle and its tires, only displays/transmits sensors for this setup. Unfiltered Displays/Transmits all sensor data without any predefined configuration.
  • Vehicle Setup: Assign a vehicle as a truck or trailer.
  • Temperature Compensation: Adjust pressure readings based on temperature.
  • RF Settings: Configure single or dual RF channels with specific sensor communication type.

For Trucks only:

  • Number of Trailers, specify the number of trailers (0–7).
  • Enable Drop and Hook, for automatic trailer detection and ordering.
  • Input Trailer MAC IDs, manually or via QR codes.

For CAN only:

  • CAN Termination: Enables or disables termination resistance, essential for maintaining signal integrity on the CAN bus.
  • CAN Speed: Selectable speeds of 250 kbps or 500 kbps, accommodating different CAN network standards.
  • CAN Address: Configurable CAN IDs to avoid conflicts and ensure proper communication in multi-device environments.

docs.title: Device Name
docs.text:
• Device Name: Matching the unique identifier of the vehicle in the fleet.
Device Menu Trailer Menu

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text:
The Diagnostics Menu provides detailed device insights:

  • Error Type: Description of detected issues.
  • Counter: Number of occureneces.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
• Refresh: Update diagnostics data with latest info .

  • Erase and Restart: Erase and restart existing diagnostics data.

    Diagnostics Menu

docs.title: Lock with PIN
docs.text:
Secure your receiver by setting a 4-digit PIN:

  • Enable PIN: Enter a PIN to lock the device.
  • Disable PIN: Clears the PIN when security is no longer needed.
  • Access: The PIN is required to unclock the device at reconnection (see below).
Locked Device

docs.title: Tap to Read
docs.text:
Retrieve sensor details like Sensor ID and Version Info.

  • NFC Reader: Tap the sensor against your phone.

  • QR Code Reader: Scan the sensor's QR code.

    Tap to Read Menu

docs.title: Update Menu
docs.text:
A red dot on the Setup or Update Menu icon indicates available firmware updates for the connected device.

  • Automatic Updates: The app fetches the latest firmware version from the server and displays the current and available firmware versions.
  • Manual Updates: Upload firmware stored on your device for custom configurations.

docs.title: Update Process
docs.text:
1. The update progress is visually displayed by a circular progress indicator.
  2. The receiver reboots and reconnects automatically upon update completion.
Update Menu Update Available

docs.title: Airline Sensor Setup
docs.text: The Airline Sensor menu allows you to configure the Airline Sensor by assigning sensors ID and warning threshold.

docs.title: Assign Airline Sensor ID
docs.text: You can set up the Airline Sensor by adding the sensor ID. There are multiple ways to input the sensor ID:

docs.title: Manually Typing
docs.text: Enter the sensor ID directly into the input field.

docs.title: Scanning QR Code
docs.text: Use the device’s camera to scan the QR code associated with the sensor, automatically inputting the sensor ID.

docs.title: Using NFC
docs.text: Tap the NFC-enabled sensor to the device to read and assign the sensor ID quickly.

docs.title: Set Warning Thresholds
docs.text: Choose the appropriate sensor type from a list of available options. This allows the system to recognize the specific capabilities and features of the assigned sensor.

docs.title: Available Thresholds
docs.text:
• Extreme Low Pressure: Critical minimum pressure below which immediate action is required.
  • Low Pressure: A warning below the optimal pressure range.
  • High Pressure: A warning for exceeding the recommended pressure range.
Airline Sensor Menu

docs.title: Edit Tire
docs.text: The Edit Tire menu allows you to configure individual tires by assigning sensors ID and type.

docs.title: Assign a Sensor to the Tire
docs.text: You can set up the sensor for the selected tire by adding the sensor ID. There are multiple ways to input the sensor ID:

docs.title: Manually Typing
docs.text: Enter the sensor ID directly into the input field.

docs.title: Scanning QR Code
docs.text: Use the device’s camera to scan the QR code associated with the sensor, automatically inputting the sensor ID.

docs.title: Using NFC (Available for NLP sensor type only)
docs.text: Tap the NFC-enabled sensor to the device to read and assign the sensor ID quickly.

docs.title: Select Sensor Type
docs.text: Choose the appropriate sensor type from a list of available options. This allows the system to recognize the specific capabilities and features of the assigned sensor.

docs.title: Displayed Tire Information
docs.text:
Fhe following details are displayed for the tire:

  • Sensor ID: The unique identifier for the assigned sensor.
  • Sensor Type: The type of sensor configured for the tire.
  • Warning Thresholds: Displays the configured thresholds (e.g., pressure, temperature) for that specific tire.
  • Position: The tire’s position on the vehicle is displayed using the ISO position standard
Edit Tire Menu

docs.title: Edit Vehicle
docs.text: When you access the Edit Vehicle function, you are guided through a step-by-step process to configure your vehicle layout and warning thresholds. Below is a detailed breakdown of the process:

docs.title: Step 1: Select Vehicle Layout
docs.text:
The first screen allows you to define your vehicle layout

  • Select the number and arrangement of tires by typing the desired configuration.
  • This layout represents the specific vehicle setup (e.g., truck or trailer) you wish to monitor.

docs.title: Step 2:  Set Warning Thresholds
docs.text: Once the vehicle layout is configured, the next screen allows you to set warning thresholds for tire monitoring:

docs.title: Available Thresholds
docs.text:
• Extreme Low Pressure: Critical minimum pressure below which immediate action is required.
  • Low Pressure: A warning for tires operating below the optimal pressure range.
  • High Pressure: A warning for tires exceeding the recommended pressure range.
  • High Temperature: Alerts for excessive tire temperatures, which could indicate overheating.

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text:
• Per Axle Configuration: Set individual threshold values for each axle based on its specific requirements.
  • Apply to All Axles: Quickly apply the same threshold values to all axles in the vehicle layout for consistency.

docs.title: R141 Support Settings
docs.text:
If the R141 Support setting is enabled, the available configurations are adjusted to comply with the regulatory requirements of the UN R141 standard:

  • You can only set the Placard Pressure (recommended tire pressure) and High Temperature Threshold.
Select Vehicle Layout Set Warning Thresholds

docs.title: Odometer Setup
docs.text: The Odometer Menu provides a straightforward way to configure the odometer for trailer vehicles. Here's a breakdown of its functionality:

docs.title: Set Odometer ID
docs.text: You can assign the odometer ID using one of the following methods:

docs.title: Manually Typing
docs.text: Enter the odometer ID directly into the input field for precise configuration.

docs.title: Scanning QR Code
docs.text:
Use the device's camera to scan the QR code associated with the odometer, allowing for quick and error-free input of the ID.

Odometer Setup Menu

docs.title: Rotate Tires
docs.text:
The Rotate function allows you to reassign the positions of sensors on the vehicle. This is useful for reconfiguring tire placements after rotation or replacement. Here's how it works:

  • The vehicle layout is displayed twice: one above the other.
  • In the upper layout, select the tire you want to move. The selected tire will be marked with a number, indicating it is ready for reassignment.
  • In the lower layout, choose the new position for the selected tire. The tire will then be moved to the chosen position, updating the sensor configuration accordingly. This process ensures a quick and accurate reassignment of sensor positions on the vehicle.
Rotate Tires Menu

docs.title: Vehicle Screen
docs.text: The Vehicle Screen dynamically adjusts its functionality based on the device communication type and the vehicle type (truck or trailer). Below are the available options for Filtered and Unfiltered devices.

docs.title: Filtered Devices
docs.text: If the device supports a Filtered mode, the following features are available:

docs.title: Edit Vehicle
docs.text:
• Use the Edit button to set up a vehicle layout (e.g., truck or trailer).
  • Configure tires by selecting them on the screen and setting warning thresholds. These settings are detailed in a separate page.

docs.title: Edit Tire
docs.text:
• After setting up the vehicle, tap each tire position to assign the sensor ID and sensor type to it.
  • Once the sensor ID is assigned to all tires, the setup is complete, and the tires turn green.

docs.title: Rotate Tires
docs.text: • The Rotate button allows you to change the positions of sensors within the vehicle layout. For example, if sensors need to be swapped between tires.

docs.title: Upload Configuration
docs.text:
• The Upload button sends the complete vehicle configuration to the server. This includes:

    • Device name
    • Warning thresholds
    • Sensor IDs
    • Sensor types

docs.title: Airline Sensor Setup
docs.text:
• Available for trailers only.
  • You can add the airline sensor ID and configure its warning thresholds (explained in detail on a separate page).

docs.title: Odotmeter Setup
docs.text:
• Available for trailers only.
  • Add the Odometer ID and configure it via a dedicated page.

docs.title: Unfiltered Devices
docs.text:
If the device supports a Unfiltered mode, the following features are available:

  • Upload Configuration
  • Odometer Setup (Available for trailers only)
Vehicle Menu Setup not complete (black tires) Setup complete (green tires) Unfiltered Devices
